pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: I see we're indexing on archive as well. Will this return archived apps as well?

If there isn't a unit test for this case, maybe we could add one

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Good catch! `findById` in `MongoModelProductRepository` filters out archived apps `(isArchived == false),` so getApp returns None for archived apps. However, the previous `indexAppById` would throw an `IllegalArgumentException` in that case rather than cleaning up the index entry. I've fixed this so that when getApp returns None (archived/deleted), we now call `deleteDocument` to remove it from the ES index instead.

pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: question: this loads all apps into memory at once. For deployments with a large number of apps, is this a concern?

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: This is a reasonable concern- at the current scale of our cloud customers, I don't see this being an issue but definitely something we should consider for our customer managed deployments. They better way would be to "stream" apps, so we'd need listApps (or a new method) to return a lazy collection, which would be a change to the AppService interface. Is it ok if I tackle this in a subsequent PR?

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: sure thing

pr_title: DOM-74899 Integrate Apps into Elastic Search

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: question: It looks like it would match everything alongside the constrained query, is it intentional? I see some of the other search methods here don't do that.

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: Yes, this is intentional. The `matchAllQuery` is used as a filter subquery in `makeSearchSourceBuilder` — it's the same pattern used by `searchFeatureViewsQuery` and `searchDatasetRwQuery`. The actual search constraint comes from the `makeConstrainedQuery` call which builds the must clause with the user's search phrase. The `matchAllQuery` filter just means "don't additionally restrict by project/environment/user" — apps are globally searchable (with post-query authorization filtering in `canUserViewApp`)

pr_title: DOM-75031 chore(Admin): Rework user admin services to support keycloak-only

comments.author: ddl-awroblicky
comments.body: I think targetUser is good but thoughts on renaming it to "idpUser"?

comments.author: ddl-martin-currao
comments.body:
_Aha! You fell into `userKeycloakHelper`'s trap._ It's not actually an IDP user.

`userKeycloakHelper.getDominoUserByKeycloakId` actually runs a `mergeUser` between the `maybeDominoUserId` and the actual `idpUser`, so the returned entity is none of them, but an updated version of any of them.

comments.author: ddl-awroblicky
comments.body: Can we call it mergedUser then?

comments.author: ddl-martin-currao
comments.body:
Hmmm. The issue is that later on it is actually further "merged" (kind of) with the result from the upgrade. 
Thus why it is called _target_, as it is whatever the target is, regardless of whether it's coming from a dominoUser or a keycloak user. Because if only the Keycloak user is present, it will be only an IDP user (without merging).

comments.author: ddl-awroblicky
comments.body: ah ok we can keep it as is then

comments.body:
did you consider the performance implications of this?
- this returns every single instance ever for the app
- then we sort and take a single one

is there some better query or pattern we can use further down the stack? like doing the sorting and "take one" on mongo side?

or even a special-purpose `"getLatestInstances(appIds: Seq[id])"` on the instance persister (run persister) so we remove the N+1 query pattern worry?

pr_title: [DOM-75323] Start and Stop Endpoints for App Versions

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
many of these args are an `Option`. example: compute env revision.

did you think about making the now-non-optional fields of an app version -- like compute env revisions -- _actually_ non optional in the `createAppVersion` and also in the `case class AppVersion` itself? 

right now it's not clear to me what is actually optional. i would have thought we're making the non-optional things not optional (of course, we need a migration to put in "resolved" fields to past versions)

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
I'm a bit hesitant to make fields not optional. We have a weird edge case with project templates that have apps where we create a dummy version with most of the fields intentionally empty (since we can't check the template itself/ new projects using the template may not necessarily have access to the environment/ hardware tier the original app does).

We do need some reasonable fallback behavior for starting an app with empty fields. I'm leaning towards preventing restarts of such versions (should be minimal after a migration), but let me know if you have other ideas.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
"leaning towards preventing restarts of such version"

makes sense to me: `case class AppVersion()` can have some optional fields to work with weird edge cases, but such versions cannot actually be "launched"/ran.

how about the new `launchVersion()` method declares the actual non-optional fields (doesnt have to be within that method, could even be some method/set/list on the AppVersion companion object for example), and validates they are present, throwing if there is some missing required field?

pr_title: [DOM-75323] Start and Stop Endpoints for App Versions

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
just a heads up, we have some performance issues with app endpoints. i think this `listApps()` endpoint in particular can get very slow if there are 100+ apps.

as customers use apps more and more, this problem will only get worse, and be a big pain point.

at some point -- hopefully before 6.3 -- we may need to do a serious refactor of at least this controller method's callstack.

i believe right now we're basically querying for all apps, sorting and limiting/paging in scala memory, then doing these N+1 queries to get versions and instances.

it will take some thought and effort on how to refactor this. example: push _all_ sorting/paging logic down into one persister method db query to do that db side instead of scala in-memory, and also do the lookups/joins app => app_version => app_instance in the same query pipeline to do _that_ db side instead of N+1 query pattern.

one step at a time though -- if for example sorting/paging db side takes care of a lot of performance issue, can defer this N+1 query problem.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: cc @ddl-bira-ignacio so you are aware of this issue

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body: Addressing these in a follow-up PR here: https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/47229

yea just re-iterating bira's point: if a version was created but no instance created from that (actually not a super unlikely scenario), then we'll get no version.

im not sure what the best fallback is:
- fallback to latest version
- fallback to latest version that has an instance
- fallback to latest version that has the latest-run instance

all those could be different

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
I'm currently using

fallback to any version that has the latest-run instance
fallback to latest (numerical) version if no instances exist

which seems to make the most sense in this context.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
this should throw for any part of the app version that is undefined but (previously, before this work) resolved at runtime. we took care of commit id, yes, but there are others: environment id, environment revision id can be None. these are resolved at runtime by looking at the project default, right? that could change over time.

to ensure "reproducible" versions, all such fields like this must receive the same "resolve" treatment if allowed to be None at any point -- need to resolve and persist the resolved thing before launching.

(and by the way, need to prevent "update unmodifiable field foo like commitId/env rev id/etc" while allowing "update modifiable field like hwt" -- we chatted about that before, to be taken care of in follow on work)

we don't know what to do about that yet. for now, it is good enough that everyone is aware of these limitations.

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body: nice documentation!

pr_title: [DOM-75531] Support reproducibility of imported projects and repos for app versions

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body:
my other comment about the docs was more high level. in general i feel like docstrings on classes or methods in a `/PROJECT/INTERFACE` kind of directory -- like `/apps/interface` -- should not talk about low level implementation that depends on _other_ projects. `/apps/interface` technically has _no idea_ about runs etc. It's only the monolith-adapter that knows about things like `ExecutionSpecificationBuilder` etc.

so im not convinced this is the best place for these docs, but im fine with it if you can't think of a better place that is closer to the point in the code where all this stuff comes together.

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: and i really do mean im fine with it! i just wanted to make the more general point clearer

comments.author: adrianrsy
comments.body:
Closer to the execution layer of things would be nice, but the edge cases are only relevant to apps for now and I'm not sure we have a good enough reproducibility story for dependencies across all execution types to make any comments generic to all of them.

I think I'll leave it in here for now.

i already linked it to the e2e test ticket and the AC there, it's something we can and should test there. https://dominodatalab.atlassian.net/browse/DOM-75049

pr_title: DOM-75428 Add tag ID filtering to listApps API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: is it ok to just return empty here if none of the tags match? 
It seems, functionally, correct to me, it's just that the warning log message suggests that might not be always expected.

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body: IMO I think we should keep this log since this case happens when there is a taxonomy service failure, not if none of the tags match. If the call succeeds but returns an empty list of entityIds, then this error won't be logged

pr_title: DOM-75428 Add tag ID filtering to listApps API

comments.author: ddl-bira-ignacio
comments.body:
question: any concerns with this being a large list of `allowedAppIds` that could make this problematic Unlike the other things in this filter, I assume a search coming from tags could return a number of ids.

Honestly I wouldn't be too worried if this is in the dozens but I also don't know how large a list of params we can pass to mongo (I'm assuming that's what this does).

CC: @ddl-ryan-connor

comments.author: ddl-aj-rossman
comments.body:
Good question- a few points:
- generally, Mongo's `$in` can handle large lists. There is a 16 MiB limit for the size of the query document, but even with thousands of AppIDs, we won't approach it
- Performance wise, we should be ok since we're querying on the app's ID, which is indexed.

pr_title: [DOM-75231|DOM-75232] Filters, sorting and pagination info for PAT's GET endpoints

comments.author: ddl-martin-currao
comments.body:
To be honest, I'm completely against handling expiringSoon as a status. 

I expect the API to return `active` if the token is active, and when I filter tokens (once we have filters) I expect `?status=active` to include those expiring soon. Considering that "soon" is a criteria defined by my  administrator, so it may be a month, I want to still be able to interact with that token as if it is functional. 

Instead, I would add another field on the response, so that it is:

```json
{
  "name": "my-token",
  // ...
  "status": "active",
  "expiringSoon": true
} 
```

----

Comment update: Realized my comment does not apply, as we are not even including it in the response.

The status enum apparently only applies to the filter, which ends up relating to this:

```
      case PatStatus.Active => BsonDocument("isValid" -> true)
      case PatStatus.ExpiringSoon => BsonDocument("isValid" -> true, "expiresAt" -> BsonDocument("$gt" -> nowBson, "$lte" -> expiringSoonLimit))
```

My bad. I see now that you mapped `active` to `isValid` and `expiringSoon` also to valid. 
We definitively will need to document it, but as the actual field in the response is called `isActive` and not status, it is not _that_ conflicting.

comments.author: ddl-juan-cistaro
comments.body:
Yes, I know this is kinda confusing, but this Statuses are the combination of both fields that are required by the UI. This will be part of the docs that we are going to create for PATs, but I haven't added this as a comment because I thought that the conditions were self-explained and if I added this as a comment in a different part of the code where the enum is define and then the conditions are changed because a future requirement the one doing that was never going to remember to change the comment in the other place and that's where everything starts falling down haha. But as for now the status align with these conditions:
  `active` - Valid token to be used.
  `expiringSoon` - Valid token to be used but its expiration time is in less than 7 days.
  `expired` - Already expired token.
  `revoked` - Token that has been revoked and is no longer valid, despite its expiration time.

pr_title: PLAT-9854: Update repo-standards-orb and use the new catalog v2 commands

comments.author: urianchang
comments.body: @coderabbitai, This is inaccurate. The orb code is located in https://github.com/cerebrotech/repo-standards-orb. The `store_catalog_check` command from the orb copies XML files from `results_path` into `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/...` and persists to workspace with root set to `/tmp/catalog`. When the `create_catalog_check_v2` command from the orb runs, it attaches the workspace root path at `/tmp/catalog`, so the files should be in `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/`.
